summary: Auth session restore reliability improvements on Windows startup.
release_notes: Fixed a startup auth timing race on Windows where secure session restore could run before pywebview API readiness, causing users to be shown logged out despite a valid stored refresh token.
Windows secure-session detection now uses host platform checks so refresh token migration/session status can run reliably during initial auth bootstrap.
This improves cold-start session resume consistency and reduces false daily sign-outs when tokens are still valid.

Fixed Time & Sales drops on some symbols (for example SAGT) by no longer filtering IB 'unreported' trade ticks; these are often legitimate prints. We continue filtering pastLimit outlier ticks in non-TBT modes.

Fixed 'Max number of market depth requests has been reached' IB error: depth subscriptions are now serialized with a lock and kept alive for the active terminal symbol rather than torn down on every listener disconnect.
Level 2 and Time & Sales panels now show a subtle 'No L2 data' / 'No T&S data' indicator when WebSocket data stops flowing, so silence is never ambiguous.
